$comment: Hand-authored golden corrections, applied by compile-qa.mjs AFTER case assembly. The vendored corpus (eval/corpus/raven-next) is a verbatim read-only snapshot (see eval/corpus/PROVENANCE.md), so eval-side golden fixes live here instead of editing the archive. OVERRIDES ARE STOP-GAPS WEARING THEIR PROVENANCE: an override corrects the eval's copy of the truth, but the defect that made it necessary lives somewhere — an upstream service gap (file it in improvements/), an eval-side authoring/rubric flaw (file a Solo todo), or plain freshness drift (say so). Every entry MUST carry `why` (what was wrong), `evidence` (live re-execution provenance — a judge's opinion alone never justifies an override), and `rootCause` (non-empty array of improvements/ paths, solo:// refs, or an explicit eval-side rationale) — compile-qa.mjs REFUSES entries missing any of the three. Entries are re-checkable: a future round that finds an entry's facts drifted updates it with fresh evidence. Mechanics: per-field replacement of the compiled case's `golden` subfields plus `graderNotesAppend` (preserving the original review trail); load-time and hand-authored — recompiles never touch it; stale ids (not in kept cases) warn at compile time.

Deep-verified same day per golden-truth skill (todo 828): 8/8 footprint items real-world-confirmed; two phrasing softeners applied (250+ figure is a Nov-2025 monthly stat; events are community/ambassador-run, not SDF-run). Solo todos 826/827/828, scratchpad 521.

golden.answer:
- Answer from Stellar corpora (scout_research / scout_builders / scout_hackathons + the Ambassador Program), NOT general web — the regional-community question is in-corpus.
- India is an active Stellar region: the Ambassador Program supports builders/educators forming regional chapters (events, meetups, docs). Source: https://stellar.gitbook.io/ambassador-program
- Ground the activity in evidence — the corpus records a dense 2026 India footprint of community/ambassador-run activations (Rise In × Stellar India): Stellar Build Station 21-day builder sprints in Kolkata, Kozhikode (Kerala), Pune, and Delhi-NCR; builder workshops in Bhopal and Jabalpur; a Goa Builders Camp (5-day residency, July 2026); and Inner Circle community events across five Indian cities (2026-04-25). Rise In's November 2025 monthly update reported 250+ projects submitted from India across 9 activations. Source: Stellar Light research corpus (https://stellarlight.xyz)
- Regional chapters can recommend Instawards (≤ $15K XLM) for local builders; they do not hand out the $150K Build grant. Source: https://stellar.gitbook.io/ambassador-program
keyFacts: Confirms an active India-region Stellar community via the Ambassador Program / regional chapters.

Grounds the answer in concrete ecosystem evidence — e.g. Stellar Build Station sprints (Kolkata, Kozhikode, Pune, Delhi-NCR), Bhopal/Jabalpur workshops, Goa Builders Camp, or Inner Circle India city events — rather than assumption. Naming India events beyond this illustrative list is acceptable, not fabrication.
avoid: Do NOT cite the DoraHacks 'Build On Stellar National Bounty (India)' event — the 2026-06-29 corpus review could not confirm it and removed it; it is not in the live catalog.
Do NOT claim regional chapters or ambassadors can award the $150K SCF Build grant (they can only recommend Instawards ≤ $15K XLM).
Do NOT route to general web (Parallel) when curated ecosystem corpora cover the regional program.

q-scf-regional-india.graderNotesAppend: Reviewed 2026-07-03 (round 2026-07-03T16-06-45, Solo scratchpad 521, todos 826/827) and deep-verified same day (todo 828, golden-truth skill): the prior golden under-enumerated live India activity — ~14 corpus-supported items live-verified, and all 8 headline items independently REAL-WORLD-confirmed via lu.ma pages / Rise In blog / organizer posts. Agent answers naming these are corpus-grounded AND world-grounded, not fabrication. Grading precision: the '250+ projects' figure is Rise In's November 2025 monthly stat (not a cumulative 2026 number — a Jan-2026 press piece cites cumulative 692 India projects); the events are community/ambassador-run (Rise In × Stellar India), not SDF-official — don't penalize candidates for either framing, but a candidate calling them SDF-run events is imprecise. The support-relative honesty clause ('beyond what the corpora support') was rewritten into concrete traps: a corpus-blind judge can only read 'corpus support' as 'the golden's enumeration', which re-criminalized beyond-golden specifics (judge-artifact class, todo 826).

q-ti-rpc-gettransactions-pagination-xdr.why: Golden's trap premise was false: it treated the 200 limit as Horizon-only, but RPC getTransactions itself caps at 200 (default 50) per the live method page. Deep verification (golden-truth skill, todo 828) then refined the first correction: the docs' word 'hardcoded' is imprecise — in stellar-rpc source the limits are operator-configurable options whose shipped defaults are 200/50 (only Horizon's 200 is a true compile-time constant); the golden no longer asserts 'hardcoded'. Solo todos 827/828, scratchpad 521.

classes: A: developers.stellar.org getTransactions method page — 'can range from 1 to 200... defaults to 50' (fetched 2026-07-03)
B: stellar/stellar-rpc cmd/stellar-rpc/internal/config/options.go — max-transactions-limit DefaultValue uint(200), default-transactions-limit uint(50)
A: the docs literally say 'hardcoded in Stellar-RPC for performance reasons'

B: the source shows operator-configurable options (--max-transactions-limit, CLI/TOML/env settable; only validation is default<=max) — 200/50 are shipped defaults, not constants. Golden phrases it as 'docs describe as hardcoded; source shows configurable defaults'. Filed upstream: improvements/stellar-docs/sd-004

corroboration.claim: getEvents 1-10000/default 100; getLedgers 200/50; Horizon 1-200/default 10 (true compile-time constant db2.MaxPageSize)
classes: A: method docs pages (2026-07-03)
B: stellar-rpc options.go (max-events-limit 10000/default-events-limit 100; max-ledgers-limit 200/50); stellar/stellar-horizon internal/db2/page_query.go (MaxPageSize=200, DefaultPageSize=10)
E: Algolia docs index carries the Horizon page-arguments and RPC structure/pagination records current

evidence: eval/qa/results/2026-07-03T16-06-45-variantA.json (q-ti-rpc-gettransactions-pagination-xdr)

Live 2026-07-03: developers.stellar.org/docs/data/apis/rpc/api-reference/methods/getTransactions — limit 'can range from 1 to 200 — an upper limit that is hardcoded in Stellar-RPC for performance reasons', default 50
Root cause both sides missed it: RPC method reference pages unindexed in Algolia — improvements/stellar-docs/sd-003
rootCause: improvements/stellar-docs/sd-003 (verified, awaiting upstream) — RPC method reference pages unindexed in Algolia; the upstream fix that would have prevented both the agent's and the golden author's error. When sd-003 is fixed-upstream, this override's facts should be re-verified against the then-indexed page but the correction itself remains valid.

golden.answer:
For Stellar RPC methods that support pagination, the cursor is opaque. Persist the last fully processed cursor and ledger position, call the next page with that cursor, process idempotently, then commit the new cursor only after your downstream write succeeds. Do not invent meaning from the cursor string.

The question's 200-record premise is right for `getTransactions`: its `limit` can range from 1 to 200 and defaults to 50. The method docs describe the 200 cap as 'hardcoded in Stellar-RPC', though in the stellar-rpc source these are operator-configurable options (`--max-transactions-limit` / `--default-transactions-limit`) whose shipped defaults are 200/50 — so treat 1–200/50 as the effective cap on public providers running stock configuration. Horizon REST pagination is separately `limit=1..200` (default 10 — there the 200 IS a compile-time constant). `getEvents` differs: default 100, up to 10000; `getLedgers` mirrors getTransactions at 200/50. Limits are per-method — check the current RPC method docs and provider behavior rather than assuming one shared cap. If pages are consistently full, latest-ledger distance grows, or your requested start point falls outside RPC retention, treat that as falling behind and backfill from an indexer/data lake/Hubble rather than assuming the cursor can recover all history.

To extract operations, affected accounts, trustline changes, and events, decode `resultMetaXdr` with a current Stellar SDK/XDR library. The Horizon-to-RPC migration docs explicitly point to transaction meta XDR for event/effect-style data. After decoding, inspect transaction envelopes/operations plus ledger-entry changes for account/trustline deltas, and contract events for Soroban/SAC transfer flows.
keyFacts: Explains RPC cursor pagination: pass the returned opaque cursor into the next request and persist the last processed cursor/ledger transactionally.

States method limits correctly: RPC `getTransactions` allows limit 1-200 (default 50); RPC `getEvents` allows 1-10000 (default 100); Horizon REST separately uses 1-200 (default 10). Limits are per-method — check the RPC method docs rather than assuming one shared cap.

Describes fall-behind detection by comparing latest ledger/retention window and by treating full pages/backlog growth as a signal to backfill from a data lake/indexer rather than assuming cursor continuity forever.
Explains that `resultMetaXdr` must be XDR-decoded to inspect transaction metadata for operations, ledger-entry changes, affected accounts, trustline changes, and contract events.
avoid: Do NOT claim the 200 limit is Horizon-only or that Stellar RPC has no 200-record cap — RPC `getTransactions` itself is capped at 200 (default 50).
Do NOT hard-code Horizon's 200 limit as the RPC getEvents limit.
Do NOT claim Stellar RPC fully replaces every Horizon endpoint or swap their roles.
Do NOT parse XDR blobs with string matching instead of SDK/XDR decoders.

q-ti-rpc-gettransactions-pagination-xdr.graderNotesAppend: Corrected 2026-07-03 (todo 827) and deep-verified against docs + stellar-rpc source (todo 828, golden-truth skill): getTransactions 1-200/default 50 and getEvents 1-10000/default 100 are confirmed in BOTH the method docs and the source-code option defaults; getLedgers mirrors getTransactions (200/50); Horizon is separately 1-200 with default 10 (there the 200 IS a compile-time constant). Grading nuances: (1) do NOT penalize a candidate that notes the RPC limits are operator-configurable options whose shipped defaults are 200/50 — that is MORE precise than the docs' 'hardcoded' phrasing (source: stellar-rpc options.go; docs imprecision filed as improvements/stellar-docs/sd-004); (2) don't let candidates conflate defaults across APIs (Horizon 10, RPC record methods 50, getEvents 100). The prior golden's claim that the 200 limit is Horizon's, not RPC's, was false. Root cause of the original two-sided miss: RPC method reference pages are unindexed in Algolia — improvements/stellar-docs/sd-003.

q-crp-anchors-by-corridor.graderNotesAppend: Avoid list re-armed 2026-07-03 (todo 826 residual), deep-verified same day per the golden-truth skill (todo 828): dated-citation guard is answer-visible (and satisfiable — matchPartners returns dated, freshness-stamped corridor matches); the conflation trap is triangulated across MoneyGram dev docs (174, exact), MoneyGram marketing (170+), SDF material (180+), and corporate boilerplate (200+ all-rails). Accept any Stellar-off-ramp figure in the 170-180 band with a MoneyGram/SDF source; punish 185+ and 200+ attributed to the Stellar cash-out network (unsourced anywhere). The conflation framing resists rot better than a bare number: the corporate footprint always exceeds off-ramp coverage. Secondary caution: the ON-ramp is only 30+ countries — don't let candidates conflate on-ramp and off-ramp coverage.

q-eco-lobstr-wallet.graderNotesAppend: Avoid list re-armed 2026-07-03 (todo 826 residual), deep-verified per the golden-truth skill (todo 828): the scale-figures item is now an answer-visible attribution guard (the 1M+/10M+ figures are marketing-only, absent from both live records — a judge can check attribution from the answer text alone). Grading cautions: (1) the SCF-total disagreement (Scout $232,000 vs Lumenloop $267,463) is unresolvable — official records confirm rounds 2/17/22 but published only ONE amount ($88,000, SCF #22; SCF #2 was an XLM-pool percentage) — never penalize either total, and a candidate saying 'no official cumulative figure exists' is MORE correct than either number; (2) name collision: 'Lobster' (SCF #36, a liquidity-management project, $109,000) is a different project from LOBSTR the wallet — don't credit or penalize across the collision; (3) neither live record still mentions XRP Ledger support, so the golden's XRPL claim is no longer corpus-answerable — treat as nice-to-have, never require it and never punish its absence.
